mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust and expanding economy, leading to higher expected corporate earnings and positively driving S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has shown consistent growth post-2010 recovery
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward Information Coefficient (IC) remains below 0.02 for a continuous 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above a key psychological and historical level of 5.0%, it signals a significant deterioration in the labor market, increasing recession risk and causing negative equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is currently 4.9%, near the trigger level
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating inflation (rising CPIAUCSL) and a rising policy rate (FEDFUNDS) creates a stagflationary environment. This simultaneously compresses corporate profit margins and increases the discount rate, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in CPIAUCSL > 0.5% AND 3-month change in FEDFUNDS > 0
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce a negative hit rate greater than baseline negative days (34.1%) over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a deceleration in real retail sales (RSAFS) is a leading indicator of consumer exhaustion and a potential economic downturn. This negative signal on consumption drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: A signal based on the 6-month change in RSAFS deflated by CPIAUCSL fails to achieve a negative Sharpe ratio over a 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong upward revisions or surprises in Industrial Production (INDPRO) indicate that the manufacturing and supply side of the economy is healthier than previously estimated, leading to upward revisions in earnings forecasts and positive short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is a component of the Index of Coincident Economic Indicators
failure_condition: The correlation between INDPRO monthly surprises and subsequent 5-day S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, unexpected drop in housing starts (HOUST) signals a rapid contraction in the highly cyclical and interest-rate-sensitive construction sector, creating a negative shock to forward-looking economic sentiment and equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing was central to the 2008 crisis
failure_condition: In instances where HOUST drops more than 10% MoM, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive more than 60% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: An extremely low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) reflects a highly accommodative monetary policy stance. This lowers borrowing costs for corporations and reduces the discount rate for future earnings, providing a persistent tailwind for equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS is 0.39%, well below the trigger
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0% is not statistically different from the average return when it is above 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals a slowdown in the overall economy. This directly implies lower aggregate corporate revenue and earnings growth, leading to negative revisions in equity valuations and negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 is the broadest measure of economic output
failure_condition: A signal based on the quarter-over-quarter change in GDPC1 fails to show a statistically significant negative correlation with 60-day forward returns over a full business cycle.
